subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Oh-My-God Particle
description: A quick, practical guide to The Oh-My-God Particle.

hook.text: In 1991, a cosmic ray hit Utah carrying the energy of a fast baseball—in a single proton. They called it the Oh-My-God particle.

buildup.text: Its energy was 300 million trillion electronvolts—far beyond anything a human-built accelerator has ever achieved.

discovery.text: The Fly's Eye detector measured it. At that energy, the proton was traveling at 99.99999999999999999999951% the speed of light.

twist.text: Physics says such particles should lose energy hitting microwave background photons. How this one survived the trip is unclear.

climax.text: Its source is unknown. No known mechanism easily explains how a single proton reaches such extreme energy.

punchline.text: One proton, baseball energy. We still don't know what launched it.

quiz.question: Why is the Oh-My-God particle scientifically puzzling?
options: Its extreme energy should have been lost during travel, and its source is unknown
It was the first particle ever detected from outside our galaxy
It traveled faster than light, violating Einstein's theory of relativity
